calendar.matters:
Verified Petition Of Guardian Ad Litem For Payment Of Fees For Services Rendered To C.M. Odgen; For Costs Advance Amd For Dischsrge As Guardian Ad Litemfor C.M. Ogden
Off Calendar Sep-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Guardian Ad Litem for Carl Melvin Ogden. Richard Canatella for Brian McDonald.

The Court stated that in order for Mr. Canatella's petition to be approved, that Mr. Park's fees needed to be included in the order for final distribution. Mr. Park stated that all of the assets were transferred to the Trustee and that he asked that the Trustee be allowed to pay his fees. Mr. Canatella stated that the proposed order provided that Mr. Park's fees will be paid out of the Trust. The Court granted the petitions (subject to review of the orders).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-04-2010 Continued To Sep-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Brian McDonald (Successor Trustee).

Mr. McDonald stated that he traced the stocks, that there were about 180 boxes of records when he took over, that there was a gap of several years, that there were two bank accounts totaling $187,000.00, and that they hired a CPA in 2007 to go over said records and try to reconstruct them but that the CPA was unable to reconstruct the gaps. In addition, he stated that there was a Special Needs Trust (SNT) established, that the funds had been distributed to the SNT pursuant to a Court order, that Ms. Rasch did not indicate going any further with this, that he could not obtain records for the three brokerage accounts, that the treasury bonds and the mutual funds/securities were not located and that it was concluded by the CPA that the amounts in the accounts seem to be consistant with what had been there through his tracing. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES

Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Continued To Apr-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch. Richard Canatella for Brian McDonald.

Mr. Canatella stated that he has the accounting, that it had been sent to Mr. Park, that he would send it to Ms. Rasch, that there were gaps in the accounting and that he would file it. The matter was continued to April 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the Petition for Final Distribution. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will be imposed against Mr. Canatella if the petition is not filed by that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella for Brian McDonald. Arthur Park Guardian Ad Litem.

Mr. Canatella stated that he was in accordance with Mr. Park's analysis and that he was fine with what ever distribution Mr. Park suggests. Mr. Park stated that he stood by the papers he submitted and that Debra Dolch consented to serve as Trustee. The Court denied the Petition for Instructions. Status on filing of the Inventory and Appraisal by Mr. Canatella was set for October 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the Petition for Final Account and for Final Distribution by Mr. Canatella was set for January 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the Petition For Approval of the Special Needs Trust by Mr. Park was set for December 7 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Canatella was directed to obtain good addresses for Martin Ogden and for Marty Ogden, and to make sure proper notice is served on all of the Heirs. The Court will review the order submitted by Mr. Park.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Continued To Sep-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-06-2009 Continued To Aug-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for Brian McDonald.

The Court stated the matter was on for a petition for final distribution/close the estate. Mr. Canatella confirmed that the decedent had five children one of which pre-deceased the decedent. In addition, Mr. Canatella stated that he had addresses for Martin Glenn Ogden and for Marty Ogden but their mail is returned to him; he has the envelopes in court today. The Court stated that it did not have a request to dispense with notice as to these two individuals and informed Mr. Canatella that he could mail notice and file an affidavit of mailing if he has their addresses. The Court inquired why it should not appoint a Guardian ad Litem for Carl Melvin Ogden. Mr. Canatella stated that the information he received from the facility is that Carl Ogden cannot get anything out of the estate because it would impair what he receives from the state; that what he gets is that the facility doesn't want anything from this estate. The Court ordered a Guardian Ad Litem appointed for Carl Melvin Ogden. The Court asked for clarification as to the residence of Carl Melvin Ogden. Mr. Canatella stated that what the Court has is the correct address. The matter was continued to August 3, 2009 for Mr. Canatella to give notice to the Martins and for a Guardian Ad Litem to be appointed for Carl Melvin Ogden.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Aguerre.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-22-2009 Continued To Jul-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Petition For Instrcutions
Off Calendar Apr-15-2009 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Richard Canatella for and with Brian McDonald.

Based on the representation of Mr. Canatella it is clear that Dr. Berghauser received notice. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern with regard to all family memebers having received notice, Carl Ogden having proper representation and stated that the holographic will was was confusing. Mr. Canatella stated that Mr. Ogden did not have representation. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). Bond was set for $188,000.00. The Court directed Mr. Canatella to file the petition for instructions within two weeks.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Jackson.
